texto: Tea drinking is a liturgy of comfort, and we partake of it everywhere in the world. It’s a ceremony of simplicity, nourishment for both the nomads in foreign teahouses and homebodies in their beds.

texto: I know, in my soul, that a love for travel is a gift and not a hindrance. It feels like a burden when the bucket list is bigger than the bank account, but a thirst for more of the world is not something to apologize for. Denying its presence feels like denying something good in me, something God put there. Wanderlust has a reputation as the epitome of unrequited love, something the young and naive chase after because they don’t yet realize it’s as futile as a dog chasing its tail. Turns out, ever-burning wanderlust is a good thing.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tsh Oxenreider: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I know, in my soul, that a love for travel is a gift and not a hindrance. It feels like a burden …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspiracional, travel pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e inspiracao.
